currentExperiment.focus: Make remaining Effect-native integration categories visible as fenced TODO/xfail evidence without turning the lab into the runtime substrate.

entries.oracle: ExecutionDescriptorRef rejects optional-field bags and under-identified async step refs.
RAWR service/plugin executable terminals expose .effect(...) only, reject .handler(...) or Promise bodies, and reject raw generator yields outside the RAWR Effect facade.
The public @rawr/sdk/effect facade rejects raw runtime constructors, raw Effect public type leaks, and the unverified Effect.pipe spelling.
Process runtime simulation resolves a registry boundary and supplies runtime-bound clients through invocation context; service calls still require .withInvocation(...).
Portable artifacts carry descriptor refs and not executable closures or descriptor tables.
Provider build returns ProviderEffectPlan, not Promise or CompiledExecutionPlan.
Profile-selected providers must cover non-optional provider resource requirements before boot.
Workflow/schedule/consumer-to-step membership must become declarative before this can be proof.
ProviderEffectPlan remains opaque until acquire/release, telemetry, error, and lowering fields are locked.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9 and 24; Effect-native addendum sections 12, 38, and 45.3
entries.oracle: One process-owned Effect managed runtime, internal EffectRuntimeAccess, Layer/Scope construction, and disposal/finalization remain runtime-substrate work, not public authoring.

entries.source: Runtime spec section 14; Effect-native addendum sections 32-35 and 45.7
entries.oracle: Process queue, pubsub, cache, and concurrency resources must be local runtime resource contracts and must not imply durable async or domain truth semantics.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 13 and 24; Effect-native addendum sections 15, 37, and 42
entries.oracle: Provider acquire/release plans lower through bootgraph/provisioning, not ProcessExecutionRuntime or CompiledExecutionPlan.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 9, 24, and 25; Effect-native addendum sections 40-42
entries.oracle: Executable and provider boundaries need explicit timeout, retry, interruption, telemetry, redaction, and error/exit metadata before this can become proof.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 5, 9, and 25; Effect-native addendum sections 8, 10, and 45.6
entries.oracle: Curated Effect composition helpers must be selected deliberately, with root pipe or value .pipe(...) spelling and no public raw runtime constructors.
Dispatcher access declaration must be decided before operation descriptors can be proven.
RuntimeResourceAccess remains an opaque narrow facade until method law is locked.
Cold route factory derivation mechanics must be stated before a positive derivation proof is accepted.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 12 and 24; Effect-native addendum section 39
entries.oracle: Native host callbacks must resolve registry boundaries and delegate to ProcessExecutionRuntime instead of running RawrEffect in adapters.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 12, 19, and 24; Effect-native addendum section 28
entries.oracle: Async host step invocation must run pre-derived step descriptors through ProcessExecutionRuntime while durable semantics stay with the async host.
Initial resource/provider catalog is planning input only and does not assert final ids.

entries.source: Runtime spec sections 13, 24, and 25; deployment spec runtime handoff sections
entries.oracle: Runtime profile config and redaction proof remains planning input until config binding and diagnostic-safe secret emission are locked.
Repo-local stale authority drift is source hygiene, not a type-spine proof.
